explanation:
The natural language requirement states that when the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode, it must be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure (Recruitment Maneuver, RM) with a single button press. Translating this, we focus on making sure that whenever PCV mode is active and the button to initiate the Recruitment Maneuver (RMButton) is pressed, the RM procedure becomes active (RM is true) in the next step, i.e., right after the button press, RM should be set. This is best expressed by the LTL template:
G ((PCVMode & RMButton) -> X RM)
which states that globally, whenever both PCV mode is active and the RMButton is activated, then in the next state RM becomes active. This captures the 'initiate with a single button' aspect linked to the initiation of RM when PCVMode is present.

The requirement states that in PCV mode, it should be possible to start a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) by pressing a single button. This maps to the atomic proposition 'PCVMode' (PCV being active) and the setting of 'RMButton' (the action to initiate RM via a button). Therefore, we express that whenever the system is in PCV mode and the RM button is pressed, the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) should become active in the next step (i.e., immediately or after a minimal delay). This is captured by the template: G((PCVMode & RMButton) -> (X RM)), meaning: 'Whenever PCV mode is active and the RM button is pushed, then in the next step the Recruitment Maneuver should become active.' The N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The requirement states that when in PCV mode, the system should be able to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure with a single button press. In atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' specifies when the ventilator is in PCV mode, and 'RMButton' refers to the GUI button for initiating RM. 'RM' denotes that the recruitment maneuver is active. The natural language implies: whenever in PCV mode and the user presses the RM button (RMButton), this should trigger the activation of the RM procedure. This is best captured by an LTL property that states: 'Globally, if PCVMode is true and RMButton is pressed, then RM shall become true in the next time step.' This matches the idea of initiation with a single button press. Therefore, G ((PCVMode & RMButton) -> X RM) is the most direct and robust encoding.

The requirement states that in PCV mode, a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) can be started with the push of a single button. Mapping this to the atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' indicates system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation mode, and 'RMButton' indicates the recruitment maneuver button is pressed. The likely system response to pressing the button is to initiate the RM ('RM'). Thus, the property we want is: whenever the system is in PCV mode and the RMButton is pushed, RM should become active immediately after (next step). This matches LTL template 41: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)). Here, bool_exp1 = PCVMode, bool_exp2 = RMButton, and bool_exp3 = RM.

The property requires that whenever the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode), it must always be possible to set the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) using a single button, which is represented by RMButton. This is expressed by the LTL formula G (PCVMode -> RMButton), meaning: whenever PCVMode is true, RMButton must also be true, that is, in PCV mode, the system shall allow initiation of the RM procedure by the GUI (e.g., by a single button push). This captures the requirement that in PCV mode, there should always be the option to start a Recruitment Maneuver with the push of a single button.

The property requires that whenever the PCV mode is active (indicated by the atomic proposition 'PCVMode'), it must always be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure (RM) using a single button (indicated by the atomic proposition 'RMButton'). This is represented using an implication: in every state, if PCV mode is active, the RM button must be available (i.e., it can be activated for the Recruitment Maneuver). Thus, the LTL formula G (PCVMode -> RMButton) ensures that throughout any execution, whenever the system is in PCV mode, the single-button RM procedure can always be initiated, directly capturing the requirement from the input natural language.

The requirement states that in PCV mode, the user must be able to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) via a single button. Mapping the atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' refers to being in PCV mode, and 'RMButton' represents when the RM is initiated by the GUI (e.g., by pressing a single button). 'RM' indicates the RM is active. The most natural LTL encoding of this requirement is: Whenever the system is in PCV mode and the RM button is pressed, this should result in activation of the RM (i.e., RM becomes true, meaning the lung recruitment maneuver is executed). Thus, we use template 41: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), where bool_exp1 = PCVMode, bool_exp2 = RMButton, bool_exp3 = RM. This ensures that every time the system is in PCV mode and the RM button is pressed, in the next time step RM becomes active (i.e., the recruitment maneuver starts as a result of the single-button press in PCV mode). This LTL property captures both the context (must be in PCV mode) and the effect (pressing the button triggers RM).

The requirement states that, in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode, it should be possible to initiate a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure with the push of a single button. In the given atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' indicates the system is in PCV mode and 'RMButton' indicates the RM is set by the GUI. The action of initiating the maneuver is captured by RMButton, and the state of the procedure being active is 'RM'. Therefore, the logical mapping is: whenever both PCVMode is active AND RMButton is pressed, it triggers that the RM procedure becomes active (RM). This matches with a standard trigger-to-effect pattern. Using the template: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3) best expresses that whenever both PCVMode is active and the user initiates (presses the RMButton), RM is triggered to become active. N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The requirement states that in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode, it should be possible to start a lung recruitment procedure, called the Recruitment Maneuver (RM), with the push of a single button. The atomic proposition 'PCVMode' captures that the system is in PCV mode, and 'RMButton' represents that the RM button is pressed by the user. 'RM' indicates the Recruitment Maneuver is active. The property should ensure that whenever the system is in PCV mode and the RM button is pressed, this leads to the RM becoming active immediately after (next time step). This is best modeled as: always, if (PCVMode & RMButton) then in the next state RM is true. This directly expresses initiation with a single button press, while in PCV mode, makes RM active. Hence, template 41 is chosen.

The requirement is that, when the ventilator is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode, it shall be possible to initiate the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure with the push of a single button. Among the atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' indicates that PCV mode is active, and 'RMButton' indicates that the Recruitment Maneuver is set by the GUI (the pushing of a button). The property should thus express that if the RMButton is pressed when in PCVMode, then (immediately) the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure becomes active. This is best represented by a template that, whenever 'PCVMode' and 'RMButton' are true together, then in the next moment 'RM' is true. This can be expressed with template 41: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), where bool_exp1 = PCVMode, bool_exp2 = RMButton, and bool_exp3 = RM. This LTL formula says: 'Globally, whenever the system is in PCV mode and the RM button is pressed, then in the next step the Recruitment Maneuver becomes active.' This captures the intended behavior described in the natural language sentence.
